summary: Rewrite .github/workflows/temper.yml off the retired `import` verb onto current check/emit verbs — distribution.md's documented two-line CI gate

per.section: The placements and their enforcement modes

edit.description: Replace the dead `temper import . --into .temper` step (the `import` subcommand no longer exists — absent from `Command` in src/main.rs, confirmed by grep) with the documented two-line job: `temper check` against the committed `.temper/` surface, plus — since this harness is SDK-emitted — a `temper emit --frozen` re-run byte-compared against the committed projections (e.g. `git diff --exit-code` after emit), the check distribution.md names as making byte-reproducibility mechanical.

acceptance: temper.yml runs only currently-existing `temper` subcommands, matches distribution.md's two-line CI description, and actually goes green on the PR that ships it (self-hosting, specs/intent.md).
notes: PR #15 flagged this drift (.flume/inbox.md 0017 sweep); live-verified 2026-07-07 — `import` is absent from src/main.rs's Command enum, so CI currently invokes a nonexistent subcommand on every PR. distribution.md names the intent (check + emit --frozen byte-compare) but not the literal shell line; pick whichever combination actually proves byte-reproducibility against this repo's own .temper/.

summary: Derive install's pinned @dtmd/temper version range from sdk/package.json itself so scaffold-vs-published API skew (moduleUrl file()) can't silently recur

edit.description: Replace the hand-typed `SDK_VERSION_RANGE` constant with a value derived from sdk/package.json's own `version` field (e.g. `include_str!("../sdk/package.json")` parsed once) so `ensure_package_json`'s written range can never independently drift from the workspace SDK's real version. Root cause of the reported skew: scaffold emits two-arg `file(import.meta.url, path)` (0009 module-relative, landed in 78a48db after the 0.0.4 publish) while the pinned `^0.0.4` still ships the published one-arg `file(path)` (verified via `npm pack @dtmd/temper@0.0.4`).

Bump `version` to the next patch (0.0.5) to reflect 78a48db's already-landed moduleUrl API change — the concrete fix for the currently-reported skew once a human publishes it; npm creds already exist (PACKAGING-CHANNELS notes, 07-05).

Update `ensure_package_json_pins_a_range_past_the_file_export` to assert the written range equals the derived value instead of a second hardcoded literal, so the test tracks the derivation rather than duplicating it.

tests.asserts: a fresh .temper/package.json's SDK dependency range is textually derived from sdk/package.json's version — no independently hand-typed literal to fall out of sync

acceptance: install.rs's SDK version pin is structurally derived from sdk/package.json (no hardcoded duplicate literal); cargo test proves it; sdk/package.json is bumped to 0.0.5, ready for a human npm publish to close the loop for real installs.
notes: Field report 2026-07-07: install --yes scaffolds two-arg file() but pins a published SDK still shipping one-arg file() — the identical ^0.0.2→^0.0.4 incident install.rs's own comment already records, recurring because nothing ties the hand-typed constant to the SDK's real released surface. Publish 0.0.5 promptly: once this lands, a from-source install writes ^0.0.5, unresolvable until published. Raw-Node-stack-in-emit-failure aside is accepted debt, out of scope.

summary: Remove Template.leaves/collections (declared, never populated/read, no consumer scoped) and document.rs's dead import_hash provenance fallback

edit.description: Remove `Template.leaves`/`Template.collections` (src/kind.rs:187-198): always constructed `Vec::new()`, never serialized to the lock, never read — declared-and-inert with no nested-member-predicate consumer scoped anywhere in the corpus, so subtraction is the honest move (0017 no-middles sweep). Update `from_kind_fact_row_lifts_declared_templates_as_inert_shape` and the `Template` doc comments; while here, correct the stale claim (~line 191-193) that `registration` shares this inert posture — it's actively read (graph.rs, install.rs, main.rs, drift.rs). Also switch `from_surface_dir_lifts_clause_fields_into_frontmatter`'s fixture (~line 1166) from the legacy `import_hash` key to `source_hash`, since it exists only to exercise the fallback removed below.

Remove the `.or_else(|| table.get("import_hash"))` legacy-key fallback in `provenance()` (~lines 500-516) — verified dead: `grep -rn import_hash .temper/` hits nothing in the live committed surface (only archive/dogfood/, out of scope), confirming the PR #15/#16 dogfood re-emit already dropped the old key. Update the doc comment; a provenance table carrying only the old key now correctly reads as malformed.

tests.asserts: the clause-fields fixture and the inert-template test both reflect the removals — source_hash-only provenance, no leaves/collections fields

acceptance: Template has no leaves/collections fields; provenance() reads only source_hash; cargo test green; import_hash absent from src/ outside git history.
notes: Routed from the 0017 no-middles sweep (.flume/inbox.md, chore 4c256d9): both facts re-verified on disk this tick — Template.leaves/collections have zero non-Vec::new() writers and zero readers repo-wide; import_hash is absent from the live .temper/ tree (present only in archive/dogfood/ and the fallback code). No corpus section names a 'nested-member predicate' to wire instead, so removal — never invented scope — is the honest routing.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.4, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
